info.name: Execute a GraphQL query or mutation

items.docs: Execute a GraphQL query or mutation against the AniList schema. The request body must include a `query` string and may include a `variables` object and an `operationName`. Queries may be made anonymously; mutations require a Bearer access token obtained via OAuth2.

params.name: client_id
params.description: The client ID of the developer application

params.name: redirect_uri
params.description: Registered redirect URI for the application

params.name: response_type
params.description: `code` for Authorization Code Grant, `token` for Implicit Grant

params.name: state
params.description: Opaque value the client can use to maintain state between request and callback

items.docs: Redirect a user to this endpoint to begin the OAuth2 Authorization Code or Implicit grant flow. The redirect URI must exactly match the URI configured on the developer application.

info.name: Exchange an authorization code for an access token

items.docs: Exchange an authorization code obtained from the authorize endpoint for an OAuth2 access token. AniList access tokens are JWTs valid for one year; refresh tokens are not supported.

info.name: Display the OAuth2 auth pin for manual entry

items.docs: Auth Pin redirect that displays an access token on a page for the user to copy and paste into a client application. Useful for clients that cannot register an HTTP or custom URI scheme redirect. Supports both Authorization Code and Implicit flows.
